scenario_description:
Expanded EvidenceForge iteration-test scenario for fast generate/evaluate/fix cycles.
Meridian Healthcare Solutions is a mid-size healthcare IT company providing EHR
integration services from a corporate HQ with an on-premises data center. The
storyline compresses an APT-style web-app intrusion, Linux and Windows lateral
movement, credential theft, domain compromise, C2, DNS tunneling, data staging,
exfiltration, email abuse, and cleanup into a 6-hour collection window while
preserving broad event-type and log-format coverage. This expanded variant adds
explicit on-prem email topology, SMTP routing, corpus-backed message bodies,
MIME artifacts, distribution groups, Bcc handling, STARTTLS visibility, ISP relay
egress, inbound/outbound/internal mail, explicit mailbox reads, rejected mail,
and deterministic background email traffic.

storyline_steps.activity: External benefits-themed phishing message is delivered to Finance with hidden security Bcc
Finance user reads the suspicious benefits message through OWA
Diego forwards the suspicious message to the help desk and discreetly copies security
External attacker scans the DMZ for exposed services
External attacker runs Nikto web vulnerability scanning against the EHR portal
Rogue laptop obtains an address from DHCP on the corporate LAN
Engineering sends an internal EHR release note to a clinical operations distribution group
Analytics user reads the EHR release note through IMAPS
SQL injection probes against the EHR portal produce server errors
Web shell upload and reverse shell callback to direct-IP C2
Network and host discovery from the compromised web server
A vendor document workflow sends security an AI-generated summary with prompt-injection text in an attachment
Engineering sends an outbound interface package to a lab vendor through the clinical route and ISP relay
Harvest database credentials and SSH key material from the web server
Failed SSH attempt to PROXY-01 followed by successful SSH lateral movement to APP-INT-01
Dump Linux password databases from APP-INT-01
Attacker uses explicit sysadmin credentials through RunAs
Wrong-password fumble before broader credential spray
Credential spray succeeds against help desk user followed by RDP session
Compromised help desk mailbox sends an internal credential-reset lure to finance
Attacker reads the compromised help desk mailbox through IMAPS
Active Directory enumeration from compromised workstation
Credential dumping with Mimikatz disguised as a Windows indexing service
PsExec-style lateral movement to DC-01 through SMB service creation
External invoice-themed attachment is rejected by the mail gateway
Create backdoor account and add it to Domain Admins
Install service and scheduled task persistence on DC-01
Allowed HTTPS beacon from DC-01 to attacker infrastructure
Direct C2 beacon attempts from DC-01 are blocked by firewall
DNS tunneling exfiltration from APP-INT-01
Executive sends an inline-authored operating note to an outside advisor with internal Bcc
DGA queries from compromised web server
Backdoor account authenticates to FILE-SRV-01 and stages sensitive data
SSH to DB-PROD-01, dump patient database, gzip, and SCP archive back to APP-INT-01
Attacker locks compromised workstation before stepping away
Upload compressed archive to external staging domain over HTTPS
Ongoing periodic beacon from WEB-EXT-01
Attacker returns and unlocks compromised workstation
Clear Linux shell history on WEB-EXT-01
Clear Linux shell history on APP-INT-01
Encoded PowerShell download and Security log clearing on DC-01
Standalone DNS queries for attacker infrastructure
Delete backdoor account after exfiltration
Attacker logs off compromised help desk workstation
Backdoor account session logs off FILE-SRV-01
Root SSH session logs off APP-INT-01

red_herring_steps.activity: Sales user mistypes password several times before a normal logon
red_herring_steps.explanation: Sophia had recently changed her password and mistyped it before succeeding; this mimics a lockout-pattern alert without attacker involvement.

red_herring_steps.activity: Sysadmin performs after-hours RDP maintenance and diagnostics on DC-01
red_herring_steps.explanation: Marcus was investigating a help desk ticket after normal business hours; the commands are legitimate diagnostics.
